key_insight: PI-MFA reconstructs continuous flow fields with B-splines while enforcing PDE and boundary constraints, so the approximation is both smooth and physically consistent.
engineering_relevance: This gives engineers a compact, differentiable surrogate for simulation data that is better suited for analysis, visualization, and downstream computation than purely data-driven interpolation.
method: Represent discrete flow data with tensor-product B-splines
Optimize spline control points to balance data fidelity and PDE residuals
Use exact analytic spline derivatives to evaluate physics constraints efficiently
evidence_signal: The abstract reports tests on 1D convection-diffusion, 2D coupled Burgers, and 2D incompressible Navier-Stokes, where PI-MFA reduced PDE residuals and improved balance-law consistency, often with lower error than standard MFA and competitive efficiency versus physics-informed neural networks.
watch_item: Performance depends on accurate PDE specification and tuning the fidelity-versus-physics tradeoff, so mismatched models or weak constraints can degrade reconstruction quality.
paper_title: A Physics-Informed B-Spline Framework for Continuous Approximation of Flow Data
